name: Harmala ibn Kahil
title: Cruel Archer
shortDescription: The ruthless archer of Umar Sa'd's army who targeted Ali Asghar's throat and Abbas's eye with poisoned three-pronged arrows.
fullStory:
Harmala ibn Kahil al-Asadi is one of the most detested figures in the Karbala event. He was an expert and infinitely cruel archer in the Kufan army who used special three-pronged arrows meant for hunting.

Harmala's name is recorded in three extremely heart-wrenching scenes on Ashura:
1. When Imam Hussain (AS) held up his infant child (Ali Asghar) asking for water, Harmala pierced the baby's throat with a three-pronged arrow.
2. During Abbas's (AS) battle, Harmala shot an arrow into the holy eye of Karbala's standard-bearer.
3. When Abdullah ibn Hasan (an eleven-year-old boy) shielded Imam Hussain (AS) in the slaughter pit, Harmala shot an arrow that pinned Abdullah's hand to his uncle's chest.

After the Karbala event, Harmala was severely cursed by Imam Sajjad (AS). Eventually, Mukhtar al-Thaqafi captured him and ordered his hands and feet to be cut off, and then he was burned in fire, fulfilling Imam Sajjad's (AS) prayer.
quoteArabic: أَصابَهُ سَهْمٌ مَسْمُومٌ لَهُ ثَلاثُ شُعَبٍ...
quote: (Maqtal report:) A poisoned arrow with three prongs hit the target...
